notes: SHIP v1.14.317 - red glow on the EXIT letters (owner in chat: 'can you put a red glow on the letters of EXIT'). ONE CSS rule: `body.rd #rd-exit .blabel{color:#ff5a4d;text-shadow:0 0 7px rgba(255,69,58,.85),0 0 16px rgba(255,69,58,.5)}` - the EXIT label text now glows red at rest (letters #ff5a4d bright red + a two-stop red halo), while keeping the .316 nav-item SHAPE (no pill, same Orbitron size/spacing/tick as COMMAND/WORK/CRASH CART). Specificity: `body.rd #rd-exit .blabel` (0,1,2,1) beats the base `#rd-botnav .blabel` dim-slate (0,1,1,0) so the red wins at rest; the arming rule `body.rd #rd-exit.arming .blabel` (0,1,3,1) still wins on hold so the gold charge-lighting is unchanged. PALETTE NOTE (flagged to owner in chat): red is OFF the ratified redesign palette - the rd house explicitly runs NO red/green and its danger/caution channel is magenta #ff2bd6; this red is kept as an EXPLICIT owner exception for the exit control (offered to swap to magenta #ff2bd6 if he wants it on-brand). Gates: whole-file brace balance 11890->11891 (+1, the new rule; balanced), all 4 inline <script> blocks vm.Script-compile clean, version triple .316->.317 lockstep, uniform LF, ?legacy=1 byte-identical (body.rd/#rd-exit-scoped; #rd-botnav redesign-only). Browser-verify pending owner. DEVICE-VERIFY (John): EXIT letters glow red at rest, still charges gold on hold, freeze/wake unchanged. NEXT: N-12 phase stepper (backlog).
